2603.23949.title: Argument Mining as a Text-to-Text Generation Task
2603.23949.paper_summary: Argument Mining(AM) aims to uncover the argumentative structures within a text. Previous methods require several subtasks, such as span identification, component classification, and relation classification. Consequently, these methods need rule-based postprocessing to derive argumentative structures from the output of each subtask. This approach adds to the complexity of the model and expands the search space of the hyperparameters. To address this difficulty, we propose a simple yet strong method based on a text-to-text generation approach using a pretrained encoder-decoder language model. Our method simultaneously generates argumentatively annotated text for spans, components, and relations, eliminating the need for task-specific postprocessing and hyperparameter tuning. Furthermore, because it is a straightforward text-to-text generation method, we can easily adapt our approach to various types of argumentative structures. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our method, as it achieves state-of-the-art performance on three different types of benchmark datasets: the Argument-annotated Essays Corpus(AAEC), AbstRCT, and the Cornell eRulemaking Corpus(CDCP)
2603.23949.paper_summary_zh: 論證挖掘（AM）旨在揭示文本中的論證結構。先前的方法需要幾個子任務，例如範圍識別、組件分類和關係分類。因此，這些方法需要基於規則的後處理，以從每個子任務的輸出中推導出論證結構。這種方法增加了模型的複雜性並擴大了超參數的搜索空間。為了解決這一困難，我們提出了一種基於預訓練編碼器-解碼器語言模型的簡單而強大的文本生成方法。我們的方法同時生成針對範圍、組件和關係的論證註釋文本，消除了對特定任務的後處理和超參數調整的需求。此外，由於這是一種簡單的文本生成方法，我們可以輕鬆地將我們的方法適應於各種類型的論證結構。實驗結果證明了我們方法的有效性，因為它在三種不同類型的基準數據集上達到了最先進的性能：論證註釋論文語料庫（AAEC）、AbstRCT 和康奈爾電子規劃語料庫（CDCP）。
2603.23949.author: Masayuki Kawarada et.al.
2603.23949.authors: Masayuki Kawarada, Tsutomu Hirao, Wataru Uchida, Masaaki Nagata
